task_tree.description: Resolve the minimum canonical app.memberships field set and valid-state boundary that may own app entry.
Verify that the resolved membership boundary excludes onboarding, enrollment, and subject fields from app-entry authority.
Append baseline ownership for canonical app.memberships above the protected slot range.
Align runtime app-entry reads and writes to canonical app.memberships only.
Verify that app.memberships is the sole app-entry authority in baseline and mounted runtime paths.
Resolve the canonical auth-subject entity above Supabase Auth and its minimum authority shape.
Verify that the resolved auth-subject boundary is separate from membership and learner-content authority.
Append baseline ownership for the canonical auth-subject entity above Supabase Auth.
Align runtime auth, onboarding, and teacher-rights surfaces to the canonical auth-subject entity instead of legacy profile logic.
Verify that auth-subject authority is canonical and teacher-rights separation is preserved.
Resolve the canonical DB-surface object map and source boundaries for discovery, detail, structure, and protected content.
Verify that the DB-surface map is deterministic and supersedes raw-table semantics.
Materialize the public user-independent DB surfaces for discovery, course detail, and lesson structure.
Align mounted public runtime reads to the canonical public DB surfaces so the public-surface gate can be re-run without raw-table bypass.
Verify that public DB surfaces remain user-independent and course_public_content is expressed only through public course detail.
Materialize the protected user-scoped lesson-content DB surface.
Verify that protected lesson-content access is enrollment and unlock bound, not membership bound.
Align mounted repositories, services, and routes to consume canonical DB surfaces instead of raw tables.
Verify that mounted read paths are surface-based end to end and do not restore raw-table authority.
Resolve the unified runtime_media expansion boundary, row model, and governed-surface coverage above the protected lesson-only projection.
Verify that the unified runtime_media boundary preserves authored identity owners and forbids alternate media truth paths.
Append baseline ownership for unified runtime_media, including course cover, visibility, state, and resolution eligibility truth.
Append the missing home-player runtime authority to unified runtime_media so mounted media alignment can proceed without inventing a second runtime truth path.
Append baseline ownership for the canonical home-player course-link inclusion substrate so downstream cleanup can consume active home-audio inclusion truth without reopening course ownership.
Append the approved profile-media runtime authority to unified runtime_media so the remaining mounted profile/community consumers can align without inventing a second source-truth domain.
Align mounted course-cover and home-player read composition to unified runtime_media for the governed surfaces already covered by canonical baseline authority.
Align the remaining mounted profile/community media consumers to unified runtime_media once dependency-valid authority exists for that non-core surface.
Verify that unified runtime_media is canonical across course cover and other governed media surfaces.
Run the append-only and substrate-only aggregate audit for the baseline-completion plan.
Run the final canonical authority completion audit across app entry, auth subject, DB surfaces, and unified runtime media.
